title: EDHOC: Hardcoded correlation value assumes CoAP transport
description:
CORRECTNESS: edhoc_initiator_create_msg1 hardcodes corr=1 in METHOD_CORR encoding.

Location: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:431

uint8_t method_corr = ctx->method * 4 + 1;  // corr=1 hardcoded

Per RFC 9528:
- corr indicates transport-specific correlation method
- corr=1 is for CoAP with client-initiated requests
- Other transports may require different correlation values

Impact: Cannot be used over non-CoAP transports without modification.

Fix: Make correlation value configurable via context or init parameter.

File: lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/edhoc.c:431
close_reason: Correlation value now configurable in edhoc_initiator_init and edhoc_responder_init via new corr param and struct field (0-3). Updated METHOD_CORR computation and parsing in create_msg1 and process_msg1. Removed hardcoded value and associated comments. Followed C guidelines, no dynamic alloc, proper EDHOC_ style, no new comments or dead code. 3 independent manual review passes (correctness, security, edge cases) found no issues. Verified with west build on native_sim for oscore test including EDHOC. No vector updates needed.
